name: Key Lime Cheesecake with Raspberry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the cookie crumbs with the melted butter and press into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Refrigerate.
2. In a large bowl, beat the cream cheese until smooth. Gradually add the sugar, beating until combined.
3. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition to ensure smoothness.
4. Stir in the grated lime peel and lime juice.
5. Pour the filling over the prepared crust.
6. Bake at 325°F for 55 to 65 minutes, or until set. To reduce cracking, place a shallow pan half full of hot water on the lower rack during baking.
7. Turn off the oven and let the cheesecake stand in the oven with the door open at least 4 inches for 30 minutes.
8. Remove from the oven and let cool to room temperature on a wire rack. Refrigerate for up to three days.
9. For the raspberry sauce, drain the raspberries and reserve the syrup. Mix the reserved syrup with cornstarch until smooth, then stir in the red currant jelly. Cook over medium heat until thickened and clear, stirring occasionally.
10. Serve cheesecake slices with the raspberry sauce.
